meta.note: STRUCTURED layer. Humans capture wins in accomplishments-log.md (prose); the AI workflow distills them here. The showcase site and the resume engine both read from this file.

profile.name: Azmi
profile.headline: Software Engineering Lead
profile.summary: Software Engineering Lead at ESGpedia (Singapore) with ~5 years across the company — IC to lead. Backend-leaning full-stack (Java/Spring Boot, React, AWS, Python/Node) in the ESG data & blockchain-backed certification registry domain (STACS / MAS Greenprint). Now managing a remote backend team across SEA timezones.
profile.domain: ESG data & certification registry; blockchain-backed (STACS / Greenprint partnership with MAS)

education.institution: Singapore University of Technology and Design (SUTD)

roles.title: Software Engineering Lead
roles.scope: 2 direct reports (backend, remote — Indonesia & Philippines); AAR lead across a 7-engineer backend group; owns sprint assignment, estimation, and dev briefing with the PM.

roles.title: Software Developer Intern (PowerX Apprenticeship)
roles.scope: PowerX Software & Development Programme; contributed to STACS Network / Greenprint ESG registry (MAS partnership).

achievements.title: Stepped into Software Engineering Lead role

star.situation: Promoted from Senior SWE to Lead, taking on direct people-management for the first time.
star.task: Establish a working rhythm with two remote reports (ID, PH) and own cross-team sprint AARs for 7 backend engineers.
star.action: Set up biweekly 1:1s (wellbeing before work-check), and an operating split with the PM: PM defines tickets, I assign + estimate + brief the dev.
star.result: 0 attrition; longer-tenured report (~9mo) now autonomous on multiple modules; newer report (~2mo) onboarding cleanly.

resume_bullets: Promoted to Software Engineering Lead managing 2 remote backend engineers (Indonesia & Philippines); own sprint planning and developer briefings with the PM, and run sprint AARs across a 7-engineer backend group.
Coached the longer-tenured direct report to autonomous ownership of multiple modules with minimal intervention; 0 attrition since stepping into the Lead role.

achievements.title: Mentored direct report via pair-designed TDDs on 2 modules

star.situation: A direct report raised a performance-review goal: drive TDDs (Technical Design Documents) for new modules, not just implement.
star.task: Convert the goal into reps — model the design process, not just review output.
star.action: Ran pair-design sessions on 2 modules — scope, structure, trade-offs and the TDD write-up done together to make the process visible.
star.result: Tracking over time: whether he produces TDDs unprompted, design quality in review, peers routing design questions to him.

resume_bullets: Translated a direct report's performance-review goal into structured practice — running pair-design sessions on 2 modules to model the Technical Design Document process and grow him from implementer toward designer-level module ownership.

achievements.title: Redesigned sprint AAR format to lift remote-team participation

star.situation: AARs used a start/stop/keep template; devs weren't contributing — friction and low value.
star.task: Make AARs genuinely useful for a remote-heavy team (SG/ID/PH).
star.action: Moved the live doc from VPN-gated Confluence to Teams; added a peer-appreciation column; added a focused 'topics to discuss' section; rotated in a light game segment.
star.result: Written contributions rose from ~5 to 10–15 bullets per AAR (≈2–3×); appreciation column became a steady source of peer recognition.

resume_bullets: Redesigned the sprint AAR format for a 7-engineer remote backend team — migrated the working doc out of VPN-gated Confluence into Teams, added a peer-appreciation column and a focused discussion section, and rotated in a light game segment — lifting written participation from ~5 to 10–15 bullets per AAR (≈2–3× more dev input).

achievements.title: AI-driven in-repo knowledge base (in progress)

star.situation: Earlier Confluence docs went stale; codebase remained the de facto source of truth but discoverability was poor.
star.task: Get knowledge closer to the code and lower the cost of keeping it current.
star.action: Running a hands-on experiment using AI to generate/maintain a knowledge-based README inside the codebase.
star.result: Too early — tracking adoption, currency, onboarding speed, and survival through a real refactor.

resume_bullets: Leading an exploration of AI-generated in-repo knowledge bases as a successor to stale Confluence docs — bringing architectural knowledge into the codebase and reducing doc-maintenance cost.

achievements.title: Architected ESG question-customisation module (per-asset + per-group scoping)

star.situation: ESG data entry used a fixed question set — every org saw the same questions regardless of relevance, making sessions noisy and slow.
star.task: Replace the fixed set so each org sees only relevant questions and work can be distributed.
star.action: Architected the module end-to-end: data model letting each asset own a curated question subset, plus an assignment layer mapping question groups to user groups; delivered backend APIs, persistence, and scoping logic.
star.result: Users see only questions relevant to their asset and group; data-entry workload distributable across the right people.

resume_bullets: Architected and delivered an ESG question-customisation module that replaced a fixed org-wide question set with per-asset curation and per-user-group assignment — showing each user only their relevant questions and letting orgs distribute data-entry work.

achievements.title: Apache Superset integration with secure S3-presigned document downloads

star.situation: Needed to surface document-backed records in Superset dashboards and allow downloads without exposing raw S3 paths.
star.task: Build a full-stack flow resolving a table row to a time-limited download while keeping S3 location private.
star.action: Superset rows keyed by document UUID; a download button calls a backend endpoint that resolves the UUID to a short-lived S3 presigned URL; client downloads directly from S3.
star.result: One-click downloads from dashboards; S3 paths stay private; backend stays lightweight (only issues URLs, no byte transfer).

resume_bullets: Built a full-stack Apache Superset ↔ S3 integration — records keyed by UUID, a backend endpoint returning short-lived presigned URLs, and direct client-to-S3 downloads — exposing documents to BI users without leaking S3 paths or routing large transfers through the backend.

achievements.title: On-the-fly emissions calculator + hot-reload cache

star.situation: Data team owned emission-factor processing; the app recomputed emissions on edit via async-on-save, forcing users to save-then-wait to see results.
star.task: Keep async-on-save correct but stop forcing a save round-trip to preview numbers.
star.action: Implemented async calculation on save for persisted consistency; built an on-the-fly (non-persisting) calculator for live previews; added hot-reload caching so upstream factor changes refresh without a restart.
star.result: Instant feedback while editing — ~3 seconds saved per update; emission-factor updates propagate without disruptive deploys.

resume_bullets: Built an on-the-fly emissions calculator and hot-reload caching layer alongside the async-on-save pipeline — letting users preview totals instantly instead of round-tripping through saves, saving ~3 seconds per update and keeping cached values fresh on upstream factor changes.

achievements.title: One-week MVP for in-house OCR (AWS Textract, Python)

star.situation: Needed in-house structured extraction from documents with no existing pattern for backend consumption.
star.task: Stand up a POC fast and design a clean integration surface.
star.action: Built the MVP in Python wrapping AWS Textract behind a custom adapter, designing the interface so the backend consumes OCR output without leaking Textract's response shape.
star.result: Working MVP in ~1 week with a clean adapter contract. (Pipeline later outsourced — Textract lacked needed language coverage — but the POC and integration design stand.)

resume_bullets: Prototyped an in-house OCR service in Python on AWS Textract within one week, designing a custom adapter to expose OCR results without coupling to vendor response shapes.

achievements.title: Rolled out Flyway across 4 DB connections (3 SQL + 1 MongoDB)

star.situation: Schema/data changes were applied via manual patching — error-prone, inconsistent across environments.
star.task: Make DB updates reproducible, deploy-tied, and auditable.
star.action: Introduced Flyway from dev outward to all 4 DB connections; standardized on versioned naming; wired migrations to auto-run on every deploy.
star.result: Manual patching eliminated; promotions no longer need a separate DB step; full version history as audit/rollback reference.

resume_bullets: Rolled out Flyway across 4 database connections (3 SQL + 1 MongoDB), standardizing versioned naming so migrations auto-run on every deploy — eliminating manual data patching and producing a version-controlled audit trail.

achievements.title: Initiated codebase & schema documentation on Confluence

star.situation: Codebase and schema docs were thin; onboarding leaned on tribal knowledge.
star.task: Externalize knowledge so others could ramp up.
star.action: Set up structured Confluence docs for components and DB schemas — the first deliberate move of knowledge into a shared place.
star.result: Became the source of truth for a stretch and cut ad-hoc questions. (Later went stale; seeded the practice that evolved into the in-repo AI knowledge-base experiment.)

resume_bullets: Initiated the first formal documentation for the codebase and database schemas on Confluence, externalizing tribal knowledge during rapid team growth and seeding the practice that later evolved into in-repo, AI-assisted docs.

achievements.title: Interviewed remote backend candidates (2-round process)

star.situation: Growing team needed help vetting remote backend candidates.
star.task: Run technical interviews and give hire/no-hire signal.
star.action: Ran a 2-round process: technical round (live coding + targeted questions) and a follow-up behavioural/fit round; assessed and fed signal to the hiring panel.
star.result: Quantify later: # interviewed, # hired still on the team.

resume_bullets: Conducted 2-round technical interviews (live coding + technical Q&A, then behavioural Q&A) for remote backend candidates, contributing to hiring decisions and helping scale the team.

achievements.title: Researched ASEAN airspace capacity & 4D flight-trajectory simulation

star.situation: ASI researched optimizing airspace management and manpower allocation across ASEAN.
star.task: Model and analyze airspace network capacity and flight behaviour.
star.action: Analyzed spatial points and simulated 4D flight trajectories based on aircraft equipment and departure times.
star.result: Fed analysis into air-traffic planning proposals. (Verify dates.)

resume_bullets: Researched ASEAN airspace network capacity and simulated 4D flight trajectories to support regional air-traffic planning optimization at SUTD's Aviation Studies Institute.
